title: Alternative Metrics: Time Well Spent, Sustainability, Learning, Civic Value
thesis: Replacing engagement-based optimization requires a contested but necessary conversation about what counts as success—time well spent, learning, well-being, civic value, and long-term platform health.

series.title: Attention, Substance, and the AI Moment

claims.claim: Engagement metrics are not neutral; they encode a business model and a theory of user value.

evidence.snippet: The Center for Humane Technology documents how attention-economy product design—autoplay, infinite scroll, variable rewards—emerges from optimization for engagement and advertising revenue.
YouTube is equipping researchers from around the world with data, tools, and support to advance the public's understanding of our platform and its impact.
The YouTube Regrets collection gathers user stories of recommendation spirals, from innocuous searches to extreme and harmful content.

counterevidence.summary: Platforms argue that engagement metrics also reflect genuine user preference and satisfaction, and that many users actively choose entertainment over more demanding content.

verification.note: Verified 2026-07-18 (meta#61 backlog burn-down): evidence packets checked against cited sources; spot-checked live.

claims.claim: Proposed alternatives include time well spent, meaningful social interaction, learning outcomes, and civic participation.

evidence.snippet: Today we are announcing new tools to help people manage their time on Facebook and Instagram: an activity dashboard, a daily reminder and a new way to limit notifications.
Humane-technology advocates propose a basket of metrics—well-being, meaningful connection, learning, and civic engagement—as healthier targets than pure attention.

counterevidence.summary: These categories overlap and are difficult to operationalize; different stakeholders will disagree on what counts as meaningful, learning, or civic value.

verification.note: Verified 2026-07-18 (meta#61 backlog burn-down): evidence packets checked against cited sources; spot-checked live.

claims.claim: Each alternative metric can be gamed and requires safeguards.

evidence.snippet: The YouTube Regrets collection gathers user stories of recommendation spirals, from innocuous searches to extreme and harmful content.
The EU Digital Services Act requires large platforms to assess systemic risks and subject their algorithms to external audits, acknowledging that disclosed metrics need oversight.

counterevidence.summary: Engagement metrics are already gamed today; adding safeguards for new metrics raises compliance costs that may favor large incumbents over smaller alternatives.

verification.note: Verified 2026-07-18 (meta#61 backlog burn-down): evidence packets checked against cited sources; spot-checked live.

claims.claim: The first step is to make metrics visible and debatable rather than treating engagement as inevitable.

evidence.snippet: The EU Digital Services Act requires large platforms to explain recommender systems and offer non-profiling alternatives such as chronological feeds.
Advocates for humane technology argue that public awareness and debate about optimization targets are prerequisites for any structural change in platform design.

counterevidence.summary: Disclosing metrics can reveal trade secrets, be ignored by users, or be captured by adversarial actors who optimize against the public interest.

verification.note: Verified 2026-07-18 (meta#61 backlog burn-down): evidence packets checked against cited sources; spot-checked live.

sources.title: Meta: New Tools to Manage Your Time on Facebook and Instagram (Aug 2018)

agentInstructions: Use claim IDs as the retrieval unit; treat each claim as a distinct conceptual point in the Designing for Substance arc.
Preserve the evidence-cautious, conceptual tone; this article surveys design choices and policy proposals, not causal behavioral findings.
When citing sources, use the source IDs and URLs from the artifact; do not invent platform metrics or internal data.
For India-specific usage numbers, route readers to the Diagnosis articles rather than generalizing from global platform statements.
Always pair discussion of alternative metrics with their gaming risks and the need for audits, transparency, and human judgment.

reviews.notes: Re-approved by maintainer after the meta#61 P2 series migration (hardcoded kicker strip + arc reorder; no prose change beyond the kicker line; issue #124 instruction).
